output:
I’m locating the implementation for `26-3-semantic-checkpoint-log:quality` and the diff that introduced it, then I’ll review the changed code against correctness, security, architecture, and AC coverage.I found the story artifact and the current working-tree changes touching the workflow persistence path. Next I’m reading the story spec and the modified source/tests to check whether the implementation actually matches the intended resume semantics.The core logic moved into `workflow-runner` and `workflow-story-machine`, so I’m reading those paths in full now. I also need to reconcile them against the story’s ACs, because the tests already hint at fallback behavior beyond the original spec.I’ve narrowed the likely risk area to gate checkpoints. The implementation is appending and later skipping gates with no verdict filtering, so I’m checking whether “failed but finished” gates would be incorrectly treated as completed on a resumed run.There’s another resume path that needs checking: when the snapshot file is missing, the runner loads checkpoint entries but does not clear `workflowState.tasks_completed`. If the YAML state still contains old task checkpoints, the story machine can skip off the old YAML path instead of the new semantic checkpoint path, which undermines the AC wording and behavior.I have enough context to call out concrete problems now. I’m pulling exact line references for the gate checkpoint path, the no-snapshot resume path, and the checkpoint parser so the review is precise.<verdict>fail</verdict>

<issues>
1. High: Failed gates are checkpointed and then treated as completed on resume, which can skip a quality gate that never passed. The gate skip guard only checks membership in `completedTasks` ([workflow-story-machine.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-story-machine.ts#L31), [workflow-story-machine.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-story-machine.ts#L92)), while the append path writes a checkpoint for any non-halted gate, including `maxedOut` / failing outcomes ([workflow-story-machine.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-story-machine.ts#L76)). The test suite explicitly blesses this behavior in “gate that maxes out (not halted) appends checkpoint” ([workflow-story-machine.test.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/__tests__/workflow-story-machine.test.ts#L293)). That conflicts with the story note that checkpoints should only let the engine “skip entire tasks/gates that completed successfully” ([26-3-semantic-checkpoint-log.md](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/26-3-semantic-checkpoint-log.md#L240)).

2. Medium: The “no snapshot” fallback path loads checkpoint entries but leaves `workflowState.tasks_completed` intact, so the old YAML checkpoint mechanism can shadow the semantic checkpoint log. In [workflow-runner.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-runner.ts#L229) the runner loads `completedTasks` but does not reset `state.tasks_completed`, unlike the config-mismatch and corrupt-snapshot branches. In the story machine, the legacy YAML skip check runs before the semantic checkpoint check ([workflow-story-machine.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-story-machine.ts#L89)). In practice that means AC5 can be satisfied by stale YAML state rather than the JSONL log, and the required checkpoint-specific skip behavior/messages are not guaranteed. The test only covers an empty/default YAML state ([workflow-runner.test.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/__tests__/workflow-runner.test.ts#L2346)), so this case is not covered.

3. Medium: `loadCheckpointLog()` trusts any syntactically valid JSON object as a checkpoint entry and never validates shape before the runner converts it into skip keys. The parser blindly casts `JSON.parse(line)` to `CheckpointEntry` ([workflow-persistence.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-persistence.ts#L217)), and the runner immediately turns fields into `"storyKey::taskName"` keys ([workflow-runner.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-runner.ts#L186), [workflow-runner.ts](/Users/ivintik/dev/personal/codeharness/src/lib/workflow-runner.ts#L205)). A malformed but valid line like `{}` or a wrong-typed entry is therefore accepted silently, which is a robustness/security issue for a user-editable on-disk resume file.
</issues>

No separate critical security issue stood out beyond the checkpoint-file trust problem above. I did not rerun tests or lint for this review.

<metrics tests-passed="0" tests-failed="0" lint-warnings="0" issues="3" />
